Simulation-calibrated ancestry calling: two-stage parameter search

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

Materials & Methods §2.4–2.5 + Results §3.1–3.2 — calibrate on simcross, validate on truth

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 9, 2026

Thesis. Ancestry-caller segmentation parameters (rrate for nNIL, rigidity for RTIGER) can be calibrated from cheap Broman simcross simulations where the donor mosaic is known truth, rather than tuned by eye on real data.

Method — a two-stage search (R/calibrate.R, driven by scripts/02_calibrate.R):

  1. Log sweep — score a coarse log-spaced grid against truth to bracket the optimum (sweep_calibrate → one shared nilHMM::caller_sweep fit + scoring).
  2. Golden-ratio refine — golden-section search inside the bracket on the continuous log10(parameter) to localize it (golden_refine).

The objective is donor-fragment Dice (reciprocal-overlap of called vs true introgression blocks) — it penalizes both missed donor and the spurious 1-marker blocks that over-fragmentation produces, unlike marker recall which saturates.

NoteCompute is separated from this note

scripts/02_calibrate.R runs the sim, calibration, and benchmark, writing summary CSVs to results/sim/ (gitignored). This note only loads and plots them. Regenerate with Rscript scripts/02_calibrate.R --generate.

Show code 
suppressMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(ggplot2)
})
SIM <- here::here("results/sim")
f <- function(x) file.path(SIM, x)
# Pinned BC2S2 params snapshot: the generic calib_params.csv gets overwritten by
# BC1S4 runs (character `design` row breaks this BC2S2 note), so read a dated copy.
CALIB <- "calib_params_bc2s2_20260706024342.csv"
have <- file.exists(f(CALIB))
if (have) {
  P <- fread(f(CALIB))
  par <- setNames(P$value, P$key)
  nnil_sw <- fread(f("nnil_rrate_sweep.csv"))
  rtig_sw <- fread(f("rtiger_rigidity_sweep.csv"))
  nnil_rf <- fread(f("nnil_rrate_refine.csv"))
  rtig_rf <- fread(f("rtiger_rigidity_refine.csv"))
  bench <- fread(f("benchmark.csv"))
  frag <- fread(f("frag_sizes.csv"))
  geno <- fread(f("geno_fractions.csv"))
}

1. The simulation and its truth (§2.4)

BC2S2, n = 1500, simcross on the maize consensus map (~1783 cM, Stahl interference m = 10 / p = 0), 50 000 markers, degraded to the skim regime (~0.43× / ~72% missing). Truth = the donor mosaic. The simulated genome composition should match the single-locus (Mendelian) expectation:

Show code 
data.table(
  metric = c("REF", "HET", "ALT", "donor dosage"),
  simulated = round(par[c("REF_truth", "HET_truth", "ALT_truth", "dosage_truth")], 4),
  expected = round(par[c("REF_expect", "HET_expect", "ALT_expect", "dosage_expect")], 4)
)
         metric simulated expected
         <char>     <num>    <num>
1:          REF    0.8413   0.8438
2:          HET    0.0630   0.0625
3:          ALT    0.0958   0.0938
4: donor dosage    0.1272   0.1250

2. Two-stage calibration

For each caller the coarse log sweep (line) brackets the optimum, and the golden-ratio refine (points) homes in; the dashed line is the refined parameter carried to the benchmark.

Show code 
calib_panel <- function(sweep, refine, star, xlab, xtrans) {
  ggplot(sweep, aes(value)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = donor_frag_dice, colour = "donor-fragment Dice")) +
    geom_line(aes(y = donor_frag_FDR, colour = "donor-fragment FDR")) +
    geom_point(data = refine, aes(value, objective, colour = "golden probes"), size = 1.6) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = star, linetype = "dashed") +
    xtrans +
    labs(x = xlab, y = "score", colour = NULL) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")
}
p_nnil <- calib_panel(nnil_sw, nnil_rf, par["rrate_star"], "nNIL rrate", scale_x_log10()) +
  ggtitle(sprintf("nNIL — refined rrate = %.2g", par["rrate_star"]))
p_rtig <- calib_panel(
  rtig_sw, rtig_rf, par["rigidity_star"], "RTIGER rigidity",
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2")
) +
  ggtitle(sprintf("RTIGER — refined rigidity = %d", as.integer(par["rigidity_star"])))
patchwork_ok <- requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)
if (patchwork_ok) {
  print(patchwork::wrap_plots(p_nnil, p_rtig))
} else {
  print(p_nnil)
  print(p_rtig)
}
Show code 
# The log-sweep curves (Dice peaks then over-fragments as FDR/breakpoints climb).
show <- function(s, pcol) {
  s[, .(
    value = signif(value, 3), donor_frag_dice = round(donor_frag_dice, 3),
    donor_frag_FDR = round(donor_frag_FDR, 3), donor_marker_recall = round(donor_marker_recall, 3),
    n_breakpoints, truth_bp, ks = round(ks_fragsize, 3)
  )]
}
cat("nNIL rrate log sweep:\n")
nNIL rrate log sweep:
Show code 
print(show(nnil_sw))
       value donor_frag_dice donor_frag_FDR donor_marker_recall n_breakpoints

 2: 3.59e-06           0.958          0.008               0.998          5973
 3: 1.29e-05           0.960          0.009               0.998          6020
 4: 4.64e-05           0.962          0.010               0.998          6077

10: 1.00e-01           0.210          0.879               0.996         33797
    truth_bp    ks
       <int> <num>
 1:     6886 0.043

 6:     6886 0.023

10:     6886 0.622

cat("\nRTIGER rigidity log sweep (powers of 2, feasibility-capped):\n")

RTIGER rigidity log sweep (powers of 2, feasibility-capped):

3. Benchmark at the calibrated parameters (§2.5, §3.2)

Each caller run at its refined optimum on a held-out set, scored vs truth:

Show code 
bench[]
          caller       param marker_macro_dice donor_marker_dice
          <char>       <num>             <num>             <num>
1:     Skim-nNIL 0.001096669             0.994             0.999
2:   Skim-RTIGER 2.000000000             0.995             0.999
3: Skim-LBimpute 5.455383236             0.993             0.998
   donor_marker_recall donor_frag_dice donor_frag_FDR ks_fragsize breakpoints
                 <num>           <num>          <num>       <num>       <int>
1:               0.998           0.966          0.011       0.023        6187
2:               0.999           0.967          0.013       0.020        6294
3:               0.997           0.979          0.006       0.023        6409
   breakpoints_truth
               <int>
1:              6886
2:              6886
3:              6886

4. Fragment-size fit (supplementary)

Donor-block size distributions vs the BC2S2 truth (the calibrated callers should track the truth’s fragment sizes; KS in the benchmark table).

Show code 
ggplot(frag, aes(mb, colour = src)) +
  stat_ecdf() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "donor block size (Mb)", y = "ECDF", title = "Fragment-size fit vs truth")

5. Genotype-frequency vs the one-locus expectation (supplementary)

Show code 
ggplot(melt(geno[, .(src, HET, ALT)], id.vars = "src"), aes(src, value)) +
  geom_violin(scale = "width") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_hline(data = data.table(
    variable = c("HET", "ALT"), y = c(par["HET_expect"], par["ALT_expect"])
  ), aes(yintercept = y), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "genome fraction", title = "Genotype frequency vs one-locus expectation (dashed)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

Next steps

  • Add the BrB regime and carry the per-source calibrated parameters to source-method-comparison.qmd.
  • Sharpen the objective’s soft plateau near the optimum with a KS or breakpoint-match tie-breaker.

```````````````````{.markdown shortcodes="false"}
---
title: "Simulation-calibrated ancestry calling: two-stage parameter search"
subtitle: "Materials & Methods §2.4–2.5 + Results §3.1–3.2 — calibrate on simcross, validate on truth"
author: "Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

**Thesis.** Ancestry-caller segmentation parameters (`rrate` for nNIL, `rigidity`
for RTIGER) can be **calibrated from cheap Broman `simcross` simulations** where
the donor mosaic is known truth, rather than tuned by eye on real data.

**Method — a two-stage search** (`R/calibrate.R`, driven by
`scripts/02_calibrate.R`):

1. **Log sweep** — score a coarse log-spaced grid against truth to *bracket* the
   optimum (`sweep_calibrate` → one shared `nilHMM::caller_sweep` fit + scoring).
2. **Golden-ratio refine** — golden-section search inside the bracket on the
   continuous `log10(parameter)` to *localize* it (`golden_refine`).

The objective is **donor-fragment Dice** (reciprocal-overlap of called vs true
introgression blocks) — it penalizes both missed donor and the spurious 1-marker
blocks that over-fragmentation produces, unlike marker recall which saturates.

::: {.callout-note title="Compute is separated from this note"}
`scripts/02_calibrate.R` runs the sim, calibration, and benchmark, writing summary
CSVs to `results/sim/` (gitignored). This note only loads and plots them.
Regenerate with `Rscript scripts/02_calibrate.R --generate`.
:::

```{r setup}
suppressMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(ggplot2)
})
SIM <- here::here("results/sim")
f <- function(x) file.path(SIM, x)
# Pinned BC2S2 params snapshot: the generic calib_params.csv gets overwritten by
# BC1S4 runs (character `design` row breaks this BC2S2 note), so read a dated copy.
CALIB <- "calib_params_bc2s2_20260706024342.csv"
have <- file.exists(f(CALIB))
if (have) {
  P <- fread(f(CALIB))
  par <- setNames(P$value, P$key)
  nnil_sw <- fread(f("nnil_rrate_sweep.csv"))
  rtig_sw <- fread(f("rtiger_rigidity_sweep.csv"))
  nnil_rf <- fread(f("nnil_rrate_refine.csv"))
  rtig_rf <- fread(f("rtiger_rigidity_refine.csv"))
  bench <- fread(f("benchmark.csv"))
  frag <- fread(f("frag_sizes.csv"))
  geno <- fread(f("geno_fractions.csv"))
}

1. The simulation and its truth (§2.4)

BC2S2, n = 1500, simcross on the maize consensus map (~1783 cM, Stahl interference m = 10 / p = 0), 50 000 markers, degraded to the skim regime (~0.43× / ~72% missing). Truth = the donor mosaic. The simulated genome composition should match the single-locus (Mendelian) expectation:

{r truth, eval=have} data.table( metric = c("REF", "HET", "ALT", "donor dosage"), simulated = round(par[c("REF_truth", "HET_truth", "ALT_truth", "dosage_truth")], 4), expected = round(par[c("REF_expect", "HET_expect", "ALT_expect", "dosage_expect")], 4) )

2. Two-stage calibration

For each caller the coarse log sweep (line) brackets the optimum, and the golden-ratio refine (points) homes in; the dashed line is the refined parameter carried to the benchmark.

{r calib-fig, eval=have, fig.width=9, fig.height=4} calib_panel <- function(sweep, refine, star, xlab, xtrans) { ggplot(sweep, aes(value)) + geom_line(aes(y = donor_frag_dice, colour = "donor-fragment Dice")) + geom_line(aes(y = donor_frag_FDR, colour = "donor-fragment FDR")) + geom_point(data = refine, aes(value, objective, colour = "golden probes"), size = 1.6) + geom_vline(xintercept = star, linetype = "dashed") + xtrans + labs(x = xlab, y = "score", colour = NULL) + theme(legend.position = "top") } p_nnil <- calib_panel(nnil_sw, nnil_rf, par["rrate_star"], "nNIL rrate", scale_x_log10()) + ggtitle(sprintf("nNIL — refined rrate = %.2g", par["rrate_star"])) p_rtig <- calib_panel( rtig_sw, rtig_rf, par["rigidity_star"], "RTIGER rigidity", scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2") ) + ggtitle(sprintf("RTIGER — refined rigidity = %d", as.integer(par["rigidity_star"]))) patchwork_ok <- requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE) if (patchwork_ok) { print(patchwork::wrap_plots(p_nnil, p_rtig)) } else { print(p_nnil) print(p_rtig) }

{r calib-tables, eval=have} # The log-sweep curves (Dice peaks then over-fragments as FDR/breakpoints climb). show <- function(s, pcol) { s[, .( value = signif(value, 3), donor_frag_dice = round(donor_frag_dice, 3), donor_frag_FDR = round(donor_frag_FDR, 3), donor_marker_recall = round(donor_marker_recall, 3), n_breakpoints, truth_bp, ks = round(ks_fragsize, 3) )] } cat("nNIL rrate log sweep:\n") print(show(nnil_sw)) cat("\nRTIGER rigidity log sweep (powers of 2, feasibility-capped):\n") print(show(rtig_sw))

3. Benchmark at the calibrated parameters (§2.5, §3.2)

Each caller run at its refined optimum on a held-out set, scored vs truth:

{r benchmark, eval=have} bench[]

4. Fragment-size fit (supplementary)

Donor-block size distributions vs the BC2S2 truth (the calibrated callers should track the truth’s fragment sizes; KS in the benchmark table).

{r fragsize, eval=have, fig.width=7, fig.height=4} ggplot(frag, aes(mb, colour = src)) + stat_ecdf() + scale_x_log10() + labs(x = "donor block size (Mb)", y = "ECDF", title = "Fragment-size fit vs truth")

5. Genotype-frequency vs the one-locus expectation (supplementary)

{r genofreq, eval=have, fig.width=7, fig.height=4} ggplot(melt(geno[, .(src, HET, ALT)], id.vars = "src"), aes(src, value)) + geom_violin(scale = "width") + facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y") + geom_hline(data = data.table( variable = c("HET", "ALT"), y = c(par["HET_expect"], par["ALT_expect"]) ), aes(yintercept = y), linetype = "dashed") + labs(x = NULL, y = "genome fraction", title = "Genotype frequency vs one-locus expectation (dashed)") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

Next steps

  • Add the BrB regime and carry the per-source calibrated parameters to source-method-comparison.qmd.
  • Sharpen the objective’s soft plateau near the optimum with a KS or breakpoint-match tie-breaker.

:::
